All right, you're back. Ready for some more awesomeness in this interview. This is a great one, this section here. Victoria starts to dig in in terms of how you actually use the power of vision focus and how vision itself allows you to close more sales. So I dig into that right now. Get ready. I'm going to do some stuff and when we're done, I will see you on the other side and I'll go for a few key distinctions and I'm going to go through with the quiz to make sure you really got it and lock it in. See you in a few minutes. In a moment I want to explore how it actually works practically. How you do this and develop your vision and develop your mind. We'll talk about this. First of all, I'm intrigued to find out how you actually use the concept of the vision, not only to improve your life, but to close sales. How do you work the vision and the vision focus with the straight line? Okay, great question. And let me explain to you how I do that. There are two ways you have to essentially make someone certain. You have to make them certain about your product logically. That's actually a good ABC it adds up that when they do the math, the numbers work. It solves that problem. It'll make their life easier in some way, make their business run better. That when they figure money in benefits out, bam, that's a great product. That's logical. The next level up is when someone is actually sold on you and your product emotionally. And essentially people don't buy on logic. They buy on emotion. So they first need to be essentially satisfied their logical brain that yes, the product makes sense. Once they're satisfied that yeah, it's a great product, then that frees them up to be moved emotionally. And the way you move someone emotionally is not through the features and the benefits, per se. It's by actually painting a picture of them using your product in the future and feeling great and feeling at ease and feeling back in control. And essentially they had some pain. They use your product, they resolve their pain and they essentially visualize themselves in the future. They see their own future, the visualization and it's good. So it's learning on the sales side to actually paint a picture in someone else's mind through something that we call future pacing and depending where you are, you've already learned a bit about future pacing, which means actually taking the sale forward and actually making them run the movie about what will be like in a year from now if using your product. It should be a great movie there. At ease they feel great, they're empowered. So that is how you essentially transfer your vision of what their life will be like to them through the straight line. So without that you're just one of these sort of logical ABC boring sales people and it's very hard to actually close if you're just closing with logic. So you want to create logic and then emotion and when you're creating emotion the key is the future pace which requires creating a vision for their future. And tell us the story about how you did this at Stratton, how you inspired those guys with your vision and your why and how you did that and went through that process. Right. Well that's a good question because I think on some level it serves as a model of exactly what you should do and also a model of what you should not do because listen obviously you know the way the strategy that I used on a daily basis through my meetings, I would give a meeting every morning and I'd give about an hour and give a meeting in the evening for another hour and I would actually talk about their lives and I would talk about their pasts, about their childhoods, about where they had come from and maybe any limiting beliefs that they had walked into my company with and explained to them that when they walked into my company and sat behind this desk and I taught them what to say, they just would listen to me. Everything in the past fell off. It was a new world, a brave new frontier and they could essentially design in their own future by having this unbelievably powerful technique namely the straight line. Essentially it was a game changer, the straight line changed everything for them. So they came in not having a skill set that they could essentially achieve with, right? So I knew both yes, the straight line is that skill set that will allow them to achieve but unless I actually make them drop the baggage and now paint an unbelievably vivid picture for their future, they'll never be able to embrace the straight line and use it on a day to day basis at the highest level. So each day I would talk about their futures. Say you know guys, you know, if you are not making X whatever the amount of dollars are, you know, you're not going to be able to essentially take the care of the people that you love, get the things in life that you want. Most of them are young guys and you want to buy a house one day. You want to build a family. You want to retire with pride and dignity. You want to be able to help your community be the guy that donates the X-ray machine to the hospital. All that sort of monetary stuff and I made them actually put pictures on their computers and write notes to themselves and you know, have daily, you know, affirmations where they would just talk about their vision because what happens is this, you know, what you say to yourself, what gets said to you every once in a while doesn't have that much impact. What gets said to you or what you say to yourself every day, day in and day out has massive impact. So I essentially taught them how to create a vision for their future and it started by identifying number one, what is the vision? What do you really want in life? You know, write it out. You know, write out two, three paragraphs or two, three pages and make it unbelievably vivid in your own mind that you have to see yourself in the future. How are you dressed? How do you act? How are you confident? Are you dignified? Are you moving and grooving? Are you like a mover and a shaker? Do you see yourself with your family? Are you living on the beach? What kind of car are you driving? What kind of schools do your kids go to? How confident are they that they don't have to worry about things like food security? Is there going to be a roof over the head? How do your parents feel now that you've been able to help them and retire them? All this, and that's just a making of various types. You know, who knows what their vision was, but the point was whatever it was to them, there's no right or wrong with vision as long as of course you're hopefully not hurting other people, right? You're going to be empowering other people on yourself, but you know what your vision is, it's personal to you, it's unique to you, but you got to write it down. Then from there, you have to then go to the next step, which is why? So yeah, I have this vision. This is what I want. Why? Write it down. Say on the deepest level, ask yourself three levels down. Why does it really matter to me? And I will tell you this, when you get to this spot, when you just know at the deepest level why you truly want, why you truly desire something, it unleashes an irresistible, unstoppable force in yourself that you can truly achieve anything. That's that force that we always hear about and personal development. You know, I'm not saying on some level I hate personal development because there's so much hokeyness about it and so much misinformation out there, but here's the truth. There is a force. You have a force inside you and the way you unleash that force, it starts by knowing where in life you really want to go and why it matters to you. And then that's step one. And step two is learning. How to harness that voice in the back of your head that self doubt, or is that voice of confidence? You know, that inner dialogue that we have that you're gonna fail, you're gonna fail. You know, that one or Jordan, your capable of greatness, you deserve to succeed. You have all the skills. So learning how to harness that voice, having that compelling vision for your future, knowing why it truly matters to you at the highest possible level. If you have all that and you learn the straight line system, meaning ABC of how do you close, how do you create certainty to another person, how do you lower their action threshold, how do you loop, so on and so forth. You will be a top risk. You can help, but get what you want in life. That's the formula. Great, right? By the way, now you know how important this competency is to really be able to create a vision for your future, focus on it and get others to focus on a vision and move them emotionally as a result of that. All right? So it's time to go for your quiz now. Make sure you go to the next step with digging deeper into your vision statement. Make sure you answer all the questions to make sure you understand everything. If not, go back and repeat a lesson, nothing wrong with that, but as soon as you're done, you got this stuff.
